title.rendered: When Would You Need a Dental Crown?

content.rendered:
While a filling may fix minor tooth decay or damage, a dental crown can solve more serious oral health issues. Unfortunately, your teeth are unable to heal themselves. A cavity will not improve on its own, and the longer it goes untreated, the more painful it will become.

The good news is that dental crowns can stop the damage caused by cavities and restore your mouth to a place of health. (Plus, they may be covered by your dental insurance!)

Figuring out when you need a dental crown can be a bit confusing. That’s why our Greenville family dental practice has compiled a list of everything you need to know about crowns.

What is a dental crown?

Dental crowns are permanent tooth-shaped “caps” that are placed over weak or damaged teeth to protect and restore them. Think of crowns like a snug hat for your tooth. The crown restores the tooth’s shape, size, strength, and appearance. 

Dental crowns can be made of metals, porcelain, resin, and ceramics. They typically don’t require special care over time other than good oral hygiene.

Why would I need a dental crown?

A dental crown is used to replace the enamel covering of your tooth. So, a tooth needs a crown when the enamel is no longer performing its proper function, usually because you’re missing a significant amount of tooth structure. 

Dental crowns are used to cover damaged, cracked, broken, or even missing teeth. You may need a dental crown if you are looking to fix any of the following:

  • Protect a large cavity or badly decaying tooth 
  • Restore a cracked, chipped, or broken tooth
  • Replace a damaged filling
  • Hold a dental bridge in place
  • Cover up a tooth for cosmetic purposes
  • Protect a tooth after a root canal

Types of dental crowns:

Your dentist may have discussed “temporary” and “permanent” crowns concerning your dental crown procedure. Here’s the difference.

What is a permanent dental crown?

A permanent dental crown is designed to be worn for decades. These types of crowns are produced in a dental laboratory. They are based on the precise measurements of your mouth. Permanent crowns mimic the shape and color of your natural teeth perfectly.

What is a temporary crown?

Temporary crowns are made to be worn for only 2-3 weeks, while a permanent crown is custom-made for you. At your first dental crown appointment, your dentist will prepare your damaged tooth for its permanent crown by removing any decay and shaping it. It’s vital that your tooth is protected after it’s prepared — that’s where a temporary crown comes in. When the permanent crown is ready, you’ll return to the dental office for a quick procedure to securely attach it.

What are the advantages and disadvantages of dental crowns?

Dental crowns are a tried and true way of restoring the appearance and function of teeth that are damaged due to injury or decay. 

If your tooth has an issue that is serious enough for a dental crown, the procedure has no downside. Dental crowns are a necessary restorative treatment to keep your mouth healthy and happy!

What is the cost of a dental crown?

The cost of a dental crown depends on your insurance coverage, dental office, and if there are any added procedures needed for your treatment. It is important to note that your insurance may not cover crowns requested for cosmetic purposes. If you’re scheduling your dental crown at Carolina Oaks Greenville, our experienced team will walk you through the cost of your treatment plan. 

Are you looking for a dental crown near you?

If you think you need a dental crown procedure in the Greenville area, Carolina Oaks is here to help. Our dentist-owned practice can provide a comfortable, natural-looking crown to restore your bite and smile. Schedule an appointment with our Greenville, SC dentists today!
